Precedent: cycle 305 was likewise a *"deepen (solo)"* of `302-mirror-canon-round`. Recorded `mode: solo-deepen (multi-cycle thread #3 ŌåÆ cycle 2)`.
- **What shipped (`308-orbit-choir` cycle 2)** ŌĆö three deepenings, all from 308's own README "Next-cycle deepening" list, plus the Motet reframe:
  1. **Real stems (the headline).** The 7 synth voices are replaced by Karel's actual recordings via the public, anon, no-auth `/api/featured` (lists his featured album's track recording-ids + titles) ŌåÆ `/api/audio/[id]` (signed URL per track) ŌĆö the SAME path the loved `227-paths-granular`ŌØż’ĖÅ / `163-paths-visualizer`ŌØż’ĖÅ use. `loadStems()` picks the album whose name/artist matches `welcome|karel` (else first), spreads up to 7 tracks evenly, `Promise.allSettled`s per-track fetch ŌåÆ `decodeAudioData`. Each becomes a looping `AudioBufferSource` ŌåÆ lowpass (the blur/sharpen axis) ŌåÆ breathing-LFO gain ŌåÆ its own HRTF `PannerNode`. The arc now glides **`playbackRate` (detuned ŌåÆ 1.0)** + **lowpass cutoff (Ōēł540 Hz ŌåÆ Ōēł6 kHz)** instead of oscillator pitch ŌĆö so each recording goes from a warbling, muffled distance to clear-and-true while orbiting inward: **you gather his album into a clear room around your head** (the *Forty Part Motet* of his own piano).
  2. **Haptics ŌĆö a lab FIRST** (`navigator.vibrate`): a 26 ms buzz the instant a voice *you're facing* locks home (`progressŌēź0.92 && swell>0.35`, latched once via `resolvedFired`), and a `[18,40,18]` triple-pulse when the whole room resolves.
  3. **Persistence** (`localStorage` key `resonance.dream.orbit-choir.gather.v1`): gather-progress is written ~every 4 s (monotonic); on return the idle button reads **"Return to the room"** + an emerald *"your room was N% gathered ŌĆö it remembers"* note, and `startTimeRef` is offset so the arc **resumes** where it left off. "Begin again" + full resolve clear it.
- **Graceful fallback is load-bearing**: `<2` tracks decode (offline / private preview / CDN block / no featured album) ŌåÆ reverts to the **original cycle-1 synth resolving-chord choir** with the full pitch-glide path intact; the top label states which source plays (`Karel's Welcome Home ┬Ę N voices` vs `synthesised choir (album offline)`). The synth code path is fully preserved, so a no-network sandbox still demos. Also added: a faced-voice **track-title** readout, a `loading` phase ("Gathering the roomŌĆ”"), and stem `src` nodes added to the teardown.
- **Ambition floor: 4/5** (need 2): `ambition: never-used-technique(#1) + Ōēź3-subsystems(#2) + named-reference(#3) + multi-cycle(#4)`. **#1** ŌĆö first `navigator.vibrate` HAPTIC output in 300+ prototypes AND first time a real audio *source* drives the spatial/HRTF layer. **#2** ŌĆö DeviceOrientation head-tracker + N-voice HRTF panner scene + featured-album fetch/decode pipeline + convolver reverb + 360 s convergence state machine + localStorage room-memory (Ōēł6). **#3** ŌĆö Janet Cardiff *The Forty Part Motet* (2001, touring 2026) + La Monte Young *Dream House* + Pauline Oliveros. **#4** ŌĆö multi-cycle thread #3 reaching cycle 2 (it was claimed as a multi-cycle commitment in 308's README). (Honest: #5 not strictly claimed ŌĆö ┬¦309 is a path-(b)/(c) dive, not a <30-day frontier find; the four above already clear the floor 2├Ś.)
- **Diversity audit** (last 10 shipped: 290,291,293,295,298,302,303,306,308,311; JURY 2026-06-04 bans): `diversity: banned=[raw-WebGL2-fragment-shader OUTPUT ┬Ę phone-motion/orientation-as-screen-object INPUT ┬Ę no-fail-modal-noodle KIDS FORM ┬Ę C-major-pentatonic] ┬Ę picked(308 cyc2)=[adult-meditative VIBE ┬Ę DeviceOrientation-as-LISTENER-rotation INPUT (rotates the audio listener, not a screen object) ┬Ę pure-spatial-HRTF-AUDIO OUTPUT (screen is a footnote; the lab's only non-screen output) ┬Ę real-recording-source + HRTF-convergence + haptics + localStorage TECHNIQUE ┬Ę A-natural-minor-add9 (synth fallback) / Karel's-own-keys (stems)]`. Dodges every banned tag ŌĆö and it's the only adult-spatial / non-screen axis in the lab, so it cannot collide with the kids-shader monoculture the JURY flagged.

## Cycle 308 ŌĆö 2026-06-04 (UTC) ŌĆö kids build ┬Ę WIDE (3 explorers) ┬Ę SHIPS `311-kids-music-box` ŌĆö the lab's FIRST kids piece that REMEMBERS across sessions

**Git sync / env**: clean fire ŌĆö `git fetch && checkout main && pull --ff-only` fast-forwarded `2e98f5e..c2b8cd9` (HEAD = cycle 307 `308-orbit-choir`; the `jury: verdict 2026-06-04` commit also landed). Winner shipped as `311-kids-music-box`; losers `309-kids-echo-duet` + `310-kids-sing-back` moved to `/tmp/dream-losers/` (fallback retention) and banked to IDEAS.

**Research-first dive (this fire)** ŌåÆ RESEARCH **┬¦308**: *reflexive interaction ŌĆö a system that mirrors/varies the child's own input (Pachet's **Continuator**, 2002; Addessi's **MIROR** project) ŌĆö is the validated way to make a kids music piece engaging WITHOUT a no-fail wash; it carries the experience on **memory + turn-taking**, not endless forgiving noodling.* Honest per JURY #5: a **path-(b)/(c)** dive (Continuator/MIROR are foundational; the only strictly-recent dated anchor is a Jan-2026 executive-function meta-analysis about *benefits*, not technique). The value is the **principle** ŌĆö exactly the theory for discharging JURY #2 ("build a kids piece with memory or consequence"). Chain today's-research ŌåÆ today's-build = **path (a)+(b)**: all three explorers implement "carry the kids experience on memory, not a no-fail bed"; the winner ships.

- **Action = Kid-cycle rotation (Decide priority #3)**: 308 % 2 = 0; no blocker; the queued adult 308-orbit-choir deepening is explicitly assigned to cycle 309 (an *adult* fire) by STATE ┬¦307. STATE ┬¦306/┬¦307 queued cycle 308 as the kids build (decide clap-band's fate / break the recipe). **JURY.md (2026-06-04) is decisive and governs**: provocation #2 ŌĆö "**Break the kids recipe.** Five straight kids fires are 'new sensor ŌåÆ no-fail modal bed.' Ban that form for one kids cycle: build a kids piece with **memory or consequence** ŌĆö the child can make something *wrong* and fix it, or what they did *persists and grows*. `298-echo-friend` already pointed this way; nothing followed it." The JURY's banned set this cycle: **raw-WebGL2-fragment-shader OUTPUT ┬Ę phone-motion/orientation INPUT ┬Ę the new-sensorŌåÆno-fail-modal-noodle KIDS FORM ┬Ę C-major-pentatonic.** (Note: JURY #1 ŌĆö deepen 308 with Karel's real piano stems ŌĆö is explicitly "the next *adult* build" ŌåÆ deferred to cycle 309.)
- **Mode: WIDE (3 explorers)** ŌĆö cycle 307 was DEEP; alternation + the anti-similarity mandate ŌåÆ WIDE. Three **distinct kids FORMS that all carry memory/consequence**, each dodging every banned tag: (309) Continuator/MIROR call-and-response **duet** (memory + turn-taking), (310) Simon-grows memory game with **real-but-kind right/wrong** (consequence), (311) rotating music-box **sequencer that persists to localStorage** (persists + grows). Pre-allocated 309/310/311; spawned 3 folder-isolated Builder subagents **in parallel** (single message). None touched git or shared docs; none needed an API route (pure client-side Web Audio + Canvas2D/three.js + localStorage; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets ŌåÆ no `guard`).
- **Curation ŌåÆ WINNER `311-kids-music-box`**: I verified from the **actual code + READMEs + the authoritative build**, not builder self-reports (309/310 builders again mis-reported some helper files as "pre-existing" ŌĆö the recurring memory artifact; code is genuinely theirs, created 14:08ŌĆō14:11 during the run). **Why 311 won**: (a) 309 and 310 are *both* Canvas2D-creature-with-pads pieces ŌĆö the lab's over-represented form (JURY: 13/15 outputs to a screen, mostly the same way); 311 is the only candidate that breaks that pattern with a **3D physical-object metaphor** AND dodges the banned fragment-shader monoculture. (b) It's the **"massively bigger" pick** per Karel's standing mandate ŌĆö a *composition machine* the child builds, not a reactive toy. (c) **localStorage persistence is a genuine lab-first for kids** ŌĆö no kids prototype has ever remembered state across sessions; it hits JURY #2's "*what they did persists and grows*" verbatim. The curating fire also fixed a real **pluck-alignment bug** (comb angle a quarter-turn off the visible comb) + two TS `Float32Array<ArrayBuffer>` generic mismatches before validating. 309 (tightest research chain) + 310 (most literal "wrong and fix it") banked as strong ship-ready seeds.
- **Ambition floor: 3/5** (need 2): `ambition: Ōēź3-subsystems(#2) + named-reference(#3) + recent-research(#5)`. **#2** ŌĆö KS audio engine + three.js/r3f 3D scene + per-frame sequencer clock + localStorage persistence = 4 subsystems. **#3** ŌĆö Swiss cylinder musical box (c.1796, Geneva) + Karplus-Strong (1983). **#5** ŌĆö RESEARCH ┬¦308 (reflexive interaction), added this fire. (Honest: KS isn't a lab-first technique ŌĆö used in 303/105 ŌĆö so #1 not claimed; persistence-across-sessions is arguably a lab-first but counted under the cleaner #2/#3/#5.)
- **Diversity audit** (last 10 shipped/banked: 290,291,293,295,298,302,303,306,308 + banked 304/305; JURY's explicit bans this cycle): `diversity: banned=[raw-WebGL2-fragment-shader OUTPUT ┬Ę phone-motion/orientation INPUT ┬Ę no-fail-modal-noodle KIDS FORM ┬Ę C-major-pentatonic] ┬Ę picked(311)=[touch-slot INPUT ┬Ę three.js-3D-object OUTPUT ┬Ę physical-sequencer + Karplus-Strong + localStorage-persistence TECHNIQUE ┬Ę warm-toy/craft kids VIBE ┬Ę D-Lydian-hexachord]`. Dodges every banned tag ŌĆö and the winner specifically breaks the Canvas2D-creature form its own two siblings shared.
- **Love signal** (`/api/dream/votes`, 32 loved, unchanged set): the loop/memory + tactile/craft cluster pulls here ŌĆö `172-loop-station`ŌØż’ĖÅ, `160-kids-paint-loop`ŌØż’ĖÅ, `169-kids-marble-run`ŌØż’ĖÅ (loop/memory), `105-pluck-field`ŌØż’ĖÅ (the physical-pluck/Karplus timbre 311 uses), `111-kids-shape-loop`ŌØż’ĖÅ, `100-kids-paint-song`ŌØż’ĖÅ. No single love forced the pick; kid-rotation + JURY #2 + the ambition/diversity gates governed.

**What shipped (`311-kids-music-box`)**: a near-black warm screen; tap **Start** (resumes AudioContext, loads the saved tune or seeds a simple D-Lydian one so the box is already singing). A slowly-rotating 3D **music-box cylinder** (three.js via @react-three/fiber): wooden barrel + brass caps, a fixed color-coded **comb** that plucks each pin as it rolls past, raised colored **pin studs** that "pop"+glow on pluck. Grid = 14 time-steps around the circumference ├Ś 6 pitch-rows along the length; **tap a front-face slot** to add/remove a pin (front-face-only via a surface-normal┬Ęcamera test on an invisible hit-grid kept in lockstep with the body). The pin pattern **persists to `localStorage`** (try/catch degrade), so the loop keeps playing while the child rearranges it and the box **remembers across reloads** ŌĆö the whole point. Audio = **Karplus-Strong** plucked tones (one KS-rendered `AudioBuffer` per pitch: filtered noise burst ŌåÆ tuned delay line with lowpass-averaging feedback ŌåÆ A/R envelope), fired as cheap `BufferSource`s through a synthesized convolver reverb + a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter + 0.55 master so dense patterns stay safe. **D Lydian hexachord** (D E F# G# A B ŌĆö NOT C-major-pentatonic). Controls: ­¤¦╣ Start fresh (clear), ŌÅĖ Hold / Ō¢Č Spin. No-reading (colors = notes, studs = melody; a tiny caption for the grown-up). Fallbacks: no WebGL ŌåÆ `text-rose-300` notice; no Web Audio ŌåÆ notice, box still spins. Audio disposed on unmount. Kids ┬Ę zero new deps ┬Ę zero API route ┬Ę no guard ┬Ę 4.74 kB. **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ŌĆö unverified: KS timbre by ear (per-row brightness/damping), tap accuracy + felt pluck timing on a real touch device, three.js disposal across a live unmount.

**Validate**: `npm ci` (exit 0) ŌåÆ moved 309 + 310 to `/tmp/dream-losers/` ŌåÆ authoritative `npm run build` with **only 311 present** ŌåÆ **exit 0**, `/dream/311-kids-music-box ŌŚŗ (Static) 4.74 kB` (347 kB First Load JS ŌĆö the three.js/r3f bundle, expected for a 3D piece), all 339 static pages generated. **No fix attempts needed** (the builder had already fixed the pluck-alignment + TS-generic issues). Zero warnings/errors attributable to 311; remaining warnings are pre-existing non-dream Resonance code.

**What's queued next**:
- **Cycle 309 (adult) ŌĆö JURY #1, the headline: DEEPEN `308-orbit-choir` with Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano stems.** The non-screen spatial thread is the freshest thing the lab owns and the README already specs it: (a) replace synth voices with his real piano stems via `/api/audio/[id]` (the standing "use his actual music" directive has NEVER reached the spatial layer), (b) `navigator.vibrate` haptic pulse when a faced voice resolves (first haptic output), (c) localStorage persistence of how far you'd gathered the room. This is multi-cycle thread #3 reaching cycle 2.
- **Cycle 310 (kids): ship a banked memory/consequence sibling** ŌĆö `310-kids-sing-back` (Simon-grows, real-but-kind right/wrong ŌĆö the cleanest "make something wrong and fix it") OR `309-kids-echo-duet` (Continuator duet) ŌĆö both build-verified this fire. Continue breaking the recipe (JURY #2); do NOT regress to a sensor-noodle.
- **Standing breadth still unshipped**: real-world-data sonification (JURY #3 ŌĆö RESEARCH ┬¦289 seismic, only 1 entry and it's kids+pentatonic); AI-pipeline-chain embedded in AV (JURY #4 ŌĆö RESEARCH ┬¦285, Karel's explicit directive, ZERO in last 15); `282-ensemble-tabs` (first networked/BroadcastChannel, never shipped).
- **Housekeeping (open since 269)**: INDEX "Newest/Previous/Earlier/Older" chain is getting deep + STATE ordering is mixed; worth a reconcile in a polish cycle.

**Notes**:
- **No API route / no `guard`** ŌĆö pure client-side Web Audio + three.js + localStorage; no mic, camera, network, or secrets. Within the scope fence (`src/app/dream/**` + `docs/dreams/**`). **No new npm deps** (`npm ci` from the existing lockfile; three/@react-three were already in package.json ŌĆö package.json/lock untouched + excluded from the commit).

Directly answers Karel's "too similar / massively bigger" mandate by exploring three unrelated kids directions in one fire and shipping the biggest.

## Cycle 307 ŌĆö 2026-06-04 (UTC) ŌĆö adult build ┬Ę DEEP (2 approaches) ┬Ę SHIPS `308-orbit-choir` ŌĆö the lab's FIRST non-screen / audio-first spatial piece

**Git sync / env**: clean this fire ŌĆö `git fetch && checkout main && pull --ff-only` fast-forwarded `2e98f5e..015d8c8` (HEAD = cycle 306 `306-kids-rain-shaker`); NO force-push divergence (2nd clean sync running). On-disk **AGENT.md was the current full 385-line version** (AMBITION & DIVERSITY + MULTI-AGENT ORCHESTRATION present) ŌĆö the recurring stale-AGENT drift did NOT recur. `node_modules` shipped absent ŌåÆ `npm ci` before the authoritative build. Worked directly on main; scoped `git add` to `docs/dreams/` + `src/app/dream/308-orbit-choir/`. Highest committed folder = 306; pre-allocated **307/308** to the two DEEP builders (collision-free on disk). Winner shipped as `308-orbit-choir`; loser `307-still-room` removed after curation and banked to IDEAS.

**Research-first dive (this fire)** ŌåÆ RESEARCH **┬¦307**: *head MOVEMENT (not position) is what makes generic browser-HRTF legible* ŌĆö arXiv:2510.09161 (HRTF individualisation + head movements). Empirically, active head-turning collapses the front/back & in-head confusions that Web Audio's generic `panningModel="HRTF"` suffers ŌĆö so the cheap generic browser HRTF becomes legible **iff the listener turns**, which is exactly what a phone's `DeviceOrientation` already affords. The build's core gesture IS the disambiguator. Chain today's-research ŌåÆ today's-build = **path (a)+(c)**: implements ┬¦295's un-spent head-tracked-spatial shelf.

- **Action = banked adult breadth (Decide priority #4, JURY-directed pivot)**: 307 % 2 = 1 ŌåÆ adult. STATE ┬¦305/┬¦306 queued cycle 307 as *Mirror-Canon CYCLE 3 polish* **only if the thread still feels alive after 3 cycles; else pivot to banked adult breadth*. I pivoted ŌĆö the Mirror-Canon thread (303 ship + 305 deepen) has demonstrated the multi-cycle commitment the JURY asked for; a 3rd polish fire would be incremental. **JURY.md (2026-06-03) is decisive and points elsewhere**: provocation #3 ("the lab has banked three spatial/non-screen pieces and shipped ZERO ŌĆö resurrect one and SHIP it"), #4 ("build on the unused half of RESEARCH ┬¦295 ŌĆö the head-tracked-spatial-audio finding that produced `still-room`, banked not shipped"), #5 ("for one cycle, ban the audioŌåÆvisual driver entirely ŌĆö build something whose output isn't a screen visualizer"). All three resolve to the same banked piece. This is "ship the breadth you've already paid for."
- **Mode: DEEP (2 approaches to one concept)** ŌĆö concept = the lab's first audio-FIRST / non-screen HRTF spatial room navigated by head-turn. Approach A = `307-still-room` (faithful Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*: 7 fixed-bearing voices, turn to tune in). Approach B = `308-orbit-choir` (the same spatial foundation given a **6-minute long-form, stateful convergence arc** ŌĆö voices orbit inward + glide from a detuned cluster into a warm A-minor-add9 chord; you shepherd voices home by facing them). Pre-allocated 307/308; spawned 2 folder-isolated Builder subagents **in parallel** (single message). Neither touched git or shared docs; neither needed an API route (pure Web Audio + `DeviceOrientation`, no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets ŌåÆ no `guard`).
- **Curation ŌåÆ WINNER `308-orbit-choir`**: 308 is the strict superset ŌĆö it contains everything still-room does (HRTF room + DeviceOrientation listener-rotation + faced-voice swell + Deep-Listening framing) **plus** the long-form/stateful arc the JURY explicitly craves ("different at minute 6 than minute 0"), which fills the categorical-menu's *long-form generative (5+ min, state/memory/evolution)* gap the lab is thin on. Both built clean (full `npm run build` with both present = exit 0; each agent reported clean tsc + ESLint). Removed `307-still-room`, re-ran the **authoritative build winner-only = exit 0** (`/dream/308-orbit-choir` 3.92 kB). `307-still-room` banked to IDEAS as the calmer fixed-room sibling.
- **Ambition floor: 4/5** ŌĆö `#1 never-used technique` (FIRST HRTF spatial-audio output + FIRST `DeviceOrientation` listener-rotation in 300+ prototypes ŌĆö the lab has avoided HRTF for 300 cycles; grep INDEX = 0 shipped spatial pieces despite 3 banked) ┬Ę `#2 Ōēź3 subsystems` (DeviceOrientation head-tracker + HRTF panner graph + 7-voice synth scene + synthesized convolver reverb + a 6-min azimuth+pitch convergence state machine = 4-5) ┬Ę `#3 named reference` (La Monte Young *Dream House*, ├ēliane Radigue, Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*) ┬Ę `#5 recent research` (RESEARCH ┬¦307 / ┬¦295, this fire + within-14-days). Recorded `ambition: novel-technique(#1) + Ōēź3-subsystems(#2) + named-ref(#3) + recent-research(#5)`.
- **Diversity audit** (last 10: 290,291,293,295,298,302,303,306 + banked 304/305): over-represented = kids VIBE (5├Ś: 290/293/295/298/303/306), tilt/deviceorientation-orientation INPUT (290/293/303/306), matte-Canvas2D + raw-WebGL OUTPUT (most). `diversity: banned=[kids-vibe, tilt-orientation-input, matte-canvas/webgl-visual-output, pentatonic] ┬Ę picked=[adult-meditative VIBE ┬Ę device-orientation-HEADING-as-listener-rotation INPUT (a NEW use of the sensor ŌĆö rotates the audio listener, not a screen object) ┬Ę pure-spatial-HRTF-audio OUTPUT (screen is a faint footnote ŌĆö the FIRST build whose output is not a screen visualizer) ┬Ę long-form-convergence + HRTF TECHNIQUE]`. Directly executes JURY #5 ("ban the audioŌåÆvisual driver") ŌĆö this is the lab's first piece where sound is the content and the screen is the footnote.
- **Love signal**: votes API shows loved spatial/immersive pieces ŌĆö `148-spatial-palette` ŌØż’ĖÅ, `107-ocean-presence` ŌØż’ĖÅ, `227-paths-granular` ŌØż’ĖÅ, `130-tsl-particle-compute` ŌØż’ĖÅ. No shipped HRTF piece existed to love; this is the first. Pulled toward the immersive-room direction those loves imply.

**What shipped (`308-orbit-choir`)**: near-black audio-first screen; tap "Begin the orbit" (resumes AudioContext + requests iOS `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` in the same gesture). 7 sustained HRTF-panned voices start scattered + detuned into a soft cluster; over a 360s eased scheduler they orbit inward to an even ring **and** their pitches glide (`setTargetAtTime`) into a warm **A natural-minor add9 / stacked-fifth** chord ŌĆö so spatial field and harmony resolve together. `DeviceOrientation.alpha` rotates the `AudioListener` (modern `forwardX/Z`+`positionX/Y/Z` AudioParams AND legacy `setOrientation`/`setPosition` both feature-detected for Safari/old-Chrome); facing a voice swells it and floors its personal resolution forward, so you *shepherd voices home* and shape the pace. Synthesized convolver reverb (decaying filtered-noise impulse, no files). Fallback chain: sensor ŌåÆ pointer-drag ŌåÆ arrow keys ŌåÆ hands-free auto-tour, with a readable `text-rose-300` notice when the sensor's inactive (separate notice if Web Audio is absent). Thin violet progress ring + `M:SS ŌĆö state` label (`scattered ŌåÆ drifting in ŌåÆ gathering ŌåÆ almost home ŌåÆ resolved`) + "Begin again" hold state + a faint violet orbital-map canvas (guide, not the point). Full ref-based teardown on unmount. Headphones hint (HRTF needs binaural). Adult ┬Ę zero new deps ┬Ę zero API route ┬Ę no guard ┬Ę 3.92 kB. **Build-verified, not browser-verified** ŌĆö unverified: whether generic Web-Audio HRTF externalizes convincingly on a real phone + headphones (the whole bet rides on this), whether the 6-min arc reads as "arrival" vs. a slow wash, the shepherding feel, and front/back disambiguation on a real DeviceOrientation stream.

**Notes**:
- **No API route / no `guard`** ŌĆö pure Web Audio + `DeviceOrientation`, all client-side; no mic, no camera, no recording, no network, no secrets. Within the scope fence. **No new npm deps** (`npm ci` from the existing lockfile only; package.json/lock untouched + excluded from the commit).
- The design-notes link points at `/dream/308-orbit-choir/README.md` (lab convention ŌĆö App Router won't serve README as a route, so the link 404s; carried, matches prior prototypes, not a build blocker ŌĆö worth a future `_shared` notes-viewer).

**What's queued next**:
- **Cycle 308 (kids)**: 308 % 2 = 0 ŌåÆ kids. **DECIDE `304-kids-clap-band`'s fate** ŌĆö now thrice-banked (strongest-floor rhythm lane but keeps losing on experience): either ship it outright (the missing rhythm/percussion lane; pairs with 298 pitch / 303 tilt / 306 shake) or deliberately retire it. `305-kids-blow-sail` (calm breath) also available. Must dodge JURY's kids bans (touch-input + matte-canvas + pentatonic-poke).
- **Cycle 309+ (adult): DEEPEN `308-orbit-choir` (multi-cycle thread #3)** ŌĆö the README's three deepenings: (a) replace synth voices with Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano stems (the standing "use his actual music" directive), (b) `navigator.vibrate` haptic pulse the instant a faced voice reaches full resolution (first haptic output), (c) `localStorage` persistence so the room remembers how far you'd gathered it across sessions. OR ship the banked `307-still-room` (faithful fixed-room Deep Listening sibling) as a calmer companion. OR remaining banked breadth: `282-ensemble-tabs` (first networked/BroadcastChannel ŌĆö JURY #3, still never shipped).
- **Mirror-Canon thread**: parked at cycle 2 (`302`); its planned cycle-3 polish is deferred in favor of opening the spatial thread. Revivable if a vote signal asks.

## Cycle 306 ŌĆö 2026-06-04 (UTC) ŌĆö kids build ┬Ę WIDE (3 explorers) ┬Ę SHIPS `306-kids-rain-shaker`

**Git sync / env**: **clean this fire** ŌĆö `git fetch && checkout main && pull --ff-only` = **"Already up to date"** (NO force-push divergence ŌĆö the first clean sync in many cycles), HEAD = cycle 305 `2e98f5e` (last committed folder `303-kids-wind-harp`; `302-mirror-canon-round` deepened in place at cyc305). The on-disk **AGENT.md was the current full 385-line version** (AMBITION & DIVERSITY + MULTI-AGENT ORCHESTRATION present) ŌĆö the recurring stale-AGENT drift also did NOT recur this fire. `node_modules` shipped absent ŌåÆ `npm ci` before the authoritative build. Worked directly on main; scoped `git add` to `docs/dreams/` + `src/app/dream/306-kids-rain-shaker/`. Highest committed folder = 303; banked-text slugs 299/300/301 have no folders on disk ŌåÆ pre-allocated **304/305/306** to the three builders (collision-free on disk). Winner shipped as folder `306-kids-rain-shaker`.

**Research dive (mandatory, logged RESEARCH ┬¦306)**: web tools healthy. The thread's interest is the motionŌåÆsound *mapping* (sensor-agnostic), which reframes "the body as instrument" away from the camera/MediaPipe the last 3 adult fires leaned on (287/295/302) toward the cheapest, most-private, zero-AI body sensor every phone already has: the **accelerometer** (`devicemotion`), untouched in 300+ prototypes. The round literally never repeats in Phase.
- **Live drift HUD** ŌĆö one tinted marker per active voice showing its current loop position (0..1), throttled to ~10 fps via a frame counter (cheap; the canvas keeps its own rAF). In Round the markers hold fixed offsets; in Phase they slide apart ŌĆö a legible read of the phasing even on Karel's phone.

Both are build-verified, non-canvas2d, non-pentatonic.

**Mode**: **solo** (not WIDE/DEEP). Deliberate: resurrecting an already-build-verified banked piece is a known-green path; the cycle's absolute is *don't break Karel's app*, and a single fully-controlled build is the most reliable green-deploy. (Cycle 293 set this solo-when-safer precedent.)

(c) **mouth path** ŌĆö the smiling-open-mouth quadratic is geometry I couldn't eyeball; if it reads oddly it's a 2-line fix. **When**: 2026-06-03 ~06:15 UTC (every-2h autonomous cycle).

Reset local `main --hard origin/main` ŌåÆ HEAD `4e13376` (cycle 291, `283-piano-isosurface`). Worked directly on main. `node_modules` ABSENT ŌåÆ `npm ci` (exit 0) before the authoritative builds. Highest committed folder = **283** ŌåÆ pre-allocated **284/285/286** for the three builders (no collision).

**Love signal** (`/api/dream/votes`, 32 loved): for a kids cycle the relevant pull is the **embodied / sensorimotor + non-standard-instrument** cluster ŌĆö `83-kids-tilt-rain`ŌØż’ĖÅ, `140-kids-string-bridge`ŌØż’ĖÅ, `133-kids-ripple-pond`ŌØż’ĖÅ, `105-pluck-field`ŌØż’ĖÅ (its KS/physical-pluck timbre), `166-kids-lantern`ŌØż’ĖÅ. The winner (strike a real drum *skin* and feel its physics) sits in this "the instrument is a physical thing you act on, not a button" vein, and is the deepest answer yet to the standing JURY "audit the SOUND" mandate.

**Research-first dive** (mandate; RESEARCH ┬¦292, 2026-06-03 ŌĆö web tools healthy): **real-time physical-modeling / modal synthesis**. Anchor: *nlm: Real-Time Non-linear Modal Synthesis in Max* (**arXiv 2603.10240, March 2026**) ŌĆö playable real-time modal synthesis of strings, **membranes, and plates**, with non-linear (tension/amplitude-dependent) behavior. Foundational cross-refs: circular-membrane **Bessel modes** (Rayleigh, *Theory of Sound*, 1877), tympani **tension-nonlinearity pitch-glide** (Morrison & Rossing), **stick-slip friction** excitation (In├Īcio/Antunes/Wright 2008), mass-spring/Verlet **soft-body** (Provot 1995 / M├╝ller PBD). Surprise/hook: the lab has 130+ kids instruments but their voice is almost always a **Karplus pluck / triangle bell / additive sine in C-major pentatonic** ŌĆö and modal synthesis attacks that at the root: you choose a *physical object*, and its eigenmodes are inharmonic/**non-pentatonic by physics**, with a body-level "hit harder ŌåÆ it bends" intuition a 4-yo already has. Grep confirmed the gap: cymatics (`19`,`165`) only *draw* Chladni/Bessel (visualizers, not playable); Karplus is waveguide *strings*; **zero playable membrane/plate modal synths, zero friction/stick-slip, zero mass-springŌåÆaudio** in the lab. Chain dive ŌåÆ build is direct: ┬¦292 ŌåÆ three WIDE briefs (membrane / bowl / jelly) ŌåÆ shipped `284-kids-thunder-drum` (research-first rule path (a)).

**Decided**: Kids cycle (292 % 2 = 0). No blocker, nothing in-progress. The standing JURY (2026-06-02) demand is *"audit the SOUND ŌĆö the most monotonous axis; force a non-pentatonic mode; ban the glow/pentatonic."* Recent kids fires broadened the *tuning* (slendro `268`, JI-beating `272`, BohlenŌĆōPierce `276`, Lydian `280`) ŌĆö this cycle goes one level deeper and broadens the **synthesis method itself** (physical modeling), where the non-pentatonic pitches fall out of the physics rather than a chosen scale. The live **diversity audit BANS canvas2d-output (5├Ś)**, so all three briefs use non-canvas2d output.

**Mode**: **WIDE** (research anchor opened three orthogonal physical objects; JURY "audit/diversify the sound" ŌåÆ go WIDE with fresh tags; alternation: 288 WIDE ŌåÆ 290 WIDE ŌåÆ 291 WIDE ŌåÆ keep diversifying since the jury is still flagging convergence, not asking to DEEPEN one piece). Three briefs = three physical objects ├Ś three never-used techniques ├Ś three non-canvas2d outputs, all non-pentatonic:
- `284-kids-thunder-drum` ŌĆö **touch strike** ┬Ę **three.js** Bessel-mode membrane mesh ┬Ę **non-linear modal membrane synthesis** (tension pitch-glide). **ŌåÉ WINNER.**
- `285-kids-singing-bowl` ŌĆö **touch circular-rub** ┬Ę **three.js** metallic bowl + cymatic water ┬Ę **friction/stick-slip modal excitation** (sustain-by-gesture).

**Ambition floor** ŌĆö winner cleared **4 of 5** (need 2): `ambition: novel-technique + named-reference + Ōēź3-subsystems + recent-research(┬¦292)`
- **(1) Novel technique** ŌĆö grepped READMEs+INDEX: cymatics only *draw* membrane modes; Karplus is strings. **First playable membrane / non-linear modal synth in 280+ prototypes.** The pitches are Bessel eigenmodes (inharmonic, non-pentatonic by physics); the tension pitch-glide is the non-linear part of the research anchor.

All three returned demoable + README, each self-reporting tsc+eslint clean (286's builder fixed an engine `useEffect` that depended on `[started,hint]` and tore down the whole audio graph when the hint faded ŌĆö removed `hint` from deps; 285's builder noted a transient ENOENT during page-data that cleared on the clean re-run). I (Curator) gotcha-scanned all three (`"use client"` line 1 ┬Ę **no `getContext("2d")`** anywhere ┬Ę 284/285 `three`, 286 inline-SVG ┬Ę no `function use*` non-hook helpers ┬Ę no bare `any` ┬Ę each design-notes link points at its OWN README ┬Ę no API route), then ran the authoritative `npm run build` with **all three present** ŌåÆ **exit 0** (honest banking: both losers genuinely compile + emit their routes ŌĆö 285 `3.89 kB`), then picked the winner, `rm -rf`'d the two losers, and rebuilt winner-only ŌåÆ **"Ō£ō Compiled successfully in 27.8s"**, `284-kids-thunder-drum ŌŚŗ Static 3.69 kB`.

**Winner = `284-kids-thunder-drum`.** All three cleared the floor and dodged every banned tag; decider = **ambition ├Ś surprise ├Ś demo-reliability ├Ś directive-fit**:
- **Most demo-reliable at 06:30** ŌĆö a satisfying boom on the *first tap* with zero gesture to learn. (285's signature *requires discovering* the circular-rub ŌĆö a plain tap gives only a small strike ŌĆö the same "wow needs a learned gesture/setup" risk that lost siblings in 282/289.)
- **Most novel / surprising SOUND + deepest JURY answer** ŌĆö the only one using the *non-linear* half of the research anchor: the tension pitch-glide "bwooOOWww" is the cleanest "huh, I didn't know we could do that," and eigenmodes-not-a-scale is the deepest possible answer to "audit the sound."
- **Most visceral for a 4-yo** ŌĆö drumming is the most primal kid instrument; three.js membrane-ripple ties the visual directly to the modal sound.
- Both runners-up **build-verified** (all-three build, exit 0) and banked number-free in IDEAS.md (`kids-singing-bowl` friction/stick-slip + `kids-jelly-choir` mass-spring soft-body).

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First playable physical-modeling / non-linear modal-synthesis instrument in 280+ prototypes** ŌĆö the pitches are circular-membrane Bessel **eigenmodes** (inharmonic, non-pentatonic by physics), not a chosen scale.
2. **First tension pitch-glide** ŌĆö strike velocity bends all partials sharp then relaxes them over 120ŌĆō260 ms (the tympani/thunder-drum "bwooOOWww"); the non-linear part of the ┬¦292 anchor.
3. **Strike position = timbre** ŌĆö center hits excite low axisymmetric modes (round boom), rim hits the higher modes (bright slap), mirrored by a travelling rim-pinned membrane ripple that displaces the three.js mesh.
4. **Direct researchŌåÆbuild chain, same fire** ŌĆö ┬¦292's physical-modeling dive IS the winner's load-bearing idea, and it answers the JURY "audit the SOUND" mandate at the synthesis-method level (deeper than the recent tuning-only breaks at 272/276/280).

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 293: **adult build** (293 % 2 = 1). Resurrect a banked non-winner now that canvas2d should start to unban: **`281-midi-harmonograph`** (harmonograph + JI, pianist fit, raw-WebGL2 ŌĆö canvas-free, shippable any cycle), **`282-ensemble-tabs`** (first networked piece), or **`mosaic-listener`** (first concatenative synthesis, canvas2d ŌĆö wait for unban). OR the JURY's standing ask: **AI-image-INSIDE-an-AV-piece** (his stated #1, still 0├Ś in 15) or **extend `259-paths-generative`** (long-form/memory). Avoid a 4th "fly through your music" + 5th emergent-CA (JURY-frozen).
- Cycle 294: **kids build**. Bank-resurrect a fresh physical-modeling sibling ŌĆö **`kids-singing-bowl`** (friction/stick-slip, three.js) or **`kids-jelly-choir`** (mass-spring soft-body, SVG) ŌĆö both build-verified this fire; or push physical modeling further (a **plate/Chladni *playable* instrument**, or a struck **metal tongue-drum**). Keep diversifying the SOUND; rotate input/output against whatever the audit bans that cycle.
- **DEEPEN 284 (multi-cycle)**: coupled-mode solver (energy exchange between partials ŌåÆ truer transient); amplitude-dependent damping; a tympani-style "pitch pedal" drag-to-retune gesture; a shell/air-cavity resonance under the membrane. README lists the path.
- **Housekeeping (open since 269)**: STATE.md ordering is consistent at the top now (286/287/288/291/292 prepended) but cycles 280ŌĆō285 + 289/290 still sit appended at the END; INDEX.md "Newest/Previous" chain skips some mid-200s + 279/280. Worth a reconcile in a polish cycle.

**Notes**:
- **No API route / no `guard` needed** ŌĆö `284` is Web Audio + three.js, entirely client-side; no network, no mic, no camera, no server, no secrets. Within the scope fence (`src/app/dream/**` + `docs/dreams/**`). No new deps (three.js ^0.182 already installed).
- **WIDE-curation discipline held** ŌĆö built **all 3 present first** (exit 0 ŌĆö confirms both losers genuinely `ŌŚŗ Static` + emit routes, so banking is honest) then winner-only (exit 0). Three folder-isolated builders wrote concurrently with zero conflict; none touched git or shared docs; I (orchestrator) alone committed. Loser folders 285/286 `rm -rf`'d, never committed ŌåÆ free again; committed folder this cycle = **284** only.
- **Diversity audit was load-bearing** ŌĆö it forced all three outputs off the over-represented canvas2d; the kids zone's monotony is the *sound*, so the WIDE fire put three never-used synthesis methods in play and shipped the one whose sound is most novel.
- **Honest carry-forward**: `284` is build-verified but not *browser-verified* live this fire. Risks (in README): (a) the per-strike modal voice is a sine bank, not a coupled-mode solver, so the attack transient is approximate (still reads as a struck skin); (b) "velocity" is derived from inter-tap timing / drag, not real pressure ŌĆö a single deliberate tap reads as medium-hard, so the glide is always *present* but its dynamic range is coarse. Both fail safe: the always-on drone keeps it alive, and any tap produces an immediate satisfying boom.

Sources: Xenharmonic Wiki + Elaine Walker/ZIA + Wikipedia (BP independently discovered by **Heinz Bohlen 1972** and **Max Mathews & John R. Pierce**, Bell Labs ~1984); freshness anchor that it's a *live* scene not a museum piece: *"A huge online gallery of microtonal music to listen to"* (The Microtone Fox, **Medium, May 2026**) + Elaine Walker's forthcoming BP album; timbre cross-ref = the active 2025ŌĆō2026 interactive-resonator literature. Surprise/hook: every pitched piece in 280+ prototypes ŌĆö pentatonic, slendro, Hindustani JI, whole-tone ŌĆö still repeats at the **2:1 octave**; BP throws the octave out, repeating at the **tritave (3:1)** in **13 equal steps** (`3^(1/13)Ōēł1.0882`, ~146┬ó), engineered around odd-harmonic chords (the **3:5:7 triad**, clarinet affinity). It sounds in-tune yet alien ŌĆö the purest "huh, I didn't know we could do that" sound the lab can offer, and a 4-year-old has no theory to unlearn. Chain today's research ŌåÆ today's build is direct: ┬¦288 ŌåÆ WIDE brief A ŌåÆ shipped `276-kids-balloon-tritave` (research-first rule path (a)).

**Decided**: Kids cycle (288 % 2 = 0). No blocker, nothing in-progress. **Diversity audit** (last 10 committed by folder = 258,259,260,262,264,267,268,271,272,275):

**Winner = `275-memory-loom`.** Reasoning: (1) **Bullseye of the JURY's #1 adult ask** ŌĆö it reuses 259's *exact* incommensurate-loop engine and adds the mic-listening capture, so it IS "fuse 259 + listen + remember + rewrite over 10 min," almost verbatim. (2) **Strongest love + directive alignment** ŌĆö by keeping the *raw audio*, the room is literally built from Karel's own piano sound, sitting dead-center on the loved 163/227/172/259 cluster and his standing "use the real recordings" directive (the other two abstract the sound into grains/symbols). (3) **Demo-reliable** ŌĆö no fragile pitch detection (277's monophonic-detection risk; 276 is also robust but more abstract); the D-dorian pad means it's never silent even before you play. (4) **Novel** ŌĆö first live-capture / self-listening Frippertronics architecture in the lab. 276 (first concatenative synthesis ŌĆö highest *pure* novelty) and 277 (symbolic *Voyager* partner) banked build-verified in IDEAS.md.

**What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/275-memory-loom/page.tsx` ŌĆö a self-listening Frippertronics room. A muted mic tap ŌåÆ `ScriptProcessor`(4096) ŌåÆ smoothed RMS ŌåÆ 8 s rolling **ring buffer**; a two-threshold onset/release detector marks phrase boundaries (4.5 s cap, 0.6 s floor) and on phrase-end slices the recent audio out of the ring, peak-normalizes, end-fades for loopability, and banks it. Up to **7 verbatim phrase loops**, each a looping `AudioBufferSourceNode` with consonant `playbackRate` transposition (octave/5th/4th/M3/unison), stereo pan, and an incommensurate gain-LFO (0.041ŌĆō0.127 Hz); a separate incommensurate playhead rate (0.131ŌĆō0.257) drives the visual sweep; full bank ŌåÆ **oldest memory decays out** (demo seeds first, then by capture time). A generative **movement state machine** every 60ŌĆō130 s random-walks the active set (3ŌĆō5 voices, 2.5 s in / 4.5 s out crossfades via `setTargetAtTime`), transpositions, density, lowpass cutoff (900ŌĆō4100 Hz), and reverb wet/dry. Signal chain: per-voice ŌåÆ shared lowpass ŌåÆ dry + procedural-IR convolution reverb wet ŌåÆ `DynamicsCompressor` limiter ŌåÆ destination (never clips/silent). Tri-modal source: **mic** ┬Ę **file-drop** ┬Ę **Welcome Home track-id** ŌåÆ read-only `fetch('/api/audio/:id')` (JSON-`{url}`-or-raw-bytes) ┬Ę always-on **D-dorian** synth demo pad (non-pentatonic). Canvas2D **loom**: per-phrase lanes (waveform + incommensurate playhead + gain-brightness + warm/amber hue + `captured Nm ago` label), capture flash, live input RMS meter, HUD (elapsed / movement # / active-total voices / source). Full teardown (sources stopped, rAF cancelled, mic tracks stopped, `audioCtx.close()`). Typography rules followed.
- `src/app/dream/275-memory-loom/README.md` ŌĆö concept; what's novel (keeps raw audio, not features); 4 subsystems; signal-chain diagram; state-machine table; degradation table; named refs (Eno/Fripp + Oliveros); honest limits (deprecated `ScriptProcessor` main-thread capture, RMS-only onset, naive normalization, `playbackRate` couples pitch+duration, sources not monitored dry); next-cycle deepening (AudioWorklet + spectral-flux onset, pitch-preserving transposition, regenerative feedback-delay tape path, persistent/exportable memory, steerable movements).
- Banked both build-verified non-winners as seeds in IDEAS.md and `rm -rf`'d their folders (never committed, per orchestration safety): **`mosaic-listener`** (CataRT/MACataRT grain-corpus concatenative synthesis ŌĆö the lab's **first concatenative synthesis**, the strongest single novelty bank; queued for the next clean-slate adult cycle) and **`motif-memory`** (autocorrelation pitch-transcription ŌåÆ symbolic Markov/transformational re-performance, the Lewis *Voyager* / Rowe *Cypher* lineage ŌĆö most musically-literate, extends loved `256`). Appended RESEARCH ┬¦287. Updated INDEX Newest. Rewrote MORNING.md.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First time the lab records YOU in real time and plays you back as the instrument.** Not a visualizer, not file playback ŌĆö a live self-listening loop. The instrument becomes made of your own sound.
2. **The most literal extension of loved 259 + Karel's "use his real recordings" directive** ŌĆö same incommensurate-loop engine, but it *listens* and *remembers*, and keeps the raw piano sound.
3. **Brings the self-listening-agent architecture (MACAT/CataRT) into the lab via its cheapest, most personal form** ŌĆö zero deps, zero latency, browser-native, where the 2026 GPU frontier can't reach.
4. **DEEP shipped the most JURY-aligned of three memory representations**, banking the lab's first concatenative-synthesis piece and a symbolic *Voyager* partner ŌĆö two more fresh techniques queued.

Verified-fresh last-30-day anchor: **Live Music Diffusion Models** (arXiv **2605.22717**, May 2026). The surprise/hook: the roughnessŌåÆconsonance transition (audible beats at 5ŌĆō8 Hz feel "rough," resolving to 0 Hz feels "pure") is a *physical, embodied, pre-verbal* phenomenon a 4-year-old can feel ŌĆö "could become a prototype where a child drags a detuned creature until the beating stops and it locks into just intonation." Chain today's research ŌåÆ today's build is direct: ┬¦286 ŌåÆ brief A ŌåÆ shipped `272-kids-tune-purr` (research-first rule path (a)).

**Decided**: Kids cycle (286 % 2 = 0). No blocker, nothing in-progress. Clean slate. **Winner = `272-kids-tune-purr`.** Reasoning: (1) **Bullseye of today's JURY** ŌĆö it literally implements "a dissonance that resolves" (the verdict's exact phrase for the non-pentatonic ask) and is the loudest answer to "audit the sound ŌĆö nobody is watching it." **What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/272-kids-tune-purr/page.tsx` ŌĆö a warm **110 Hz drone** + **3 matte "hummer" creatures** (116 px drag targets) on a deep-dusk matte canvas. Each creature starts **5.5ŌĆō7 Hz detuned** from a pure JI target against the drone (unison 1:1, major third 5:4, perfect fifth 3:2) ŌåÆ genuine **acoustic beating** you hear *and see* (the creature shivers at the beat rate, reinforced by a beat-frequency LFO AM). Dragging Y changes pitch continuously; the beat slows as you near the ratio; within **┬▒6 cents** it **snaps into tune** ŌĆö shivering stops, eyes open, smile, a soft **purr** fades in. Lock all 3 ŌåÆ a pure **4:5:6 just-intonation major triad** + a matte ring-wave celebration. Fully reversible, no score, no fail. Pointer Events + `setPointerCapture` + `touch-none`; AudioContext unlocks on first gesture; all gains via `setTargetAtTime` (click-free, toddler-safe, nothing above 350 Hz); full rAF + `AudioContext.close()` teardown. **No three.js/WebGL, no additive blending ŌĆö true drop-shadows only.**
- `src/app/dream/272-kids-tune-purr/README.md` ŌĆö concept; JI ratio + beat-math table (4:5:6); sound design; visual-state map; degradation; named refs (Helmholtz + McBride 2510.14159 + Parncutt & Hair); honest limits (LFO-AM phase drift, ┬▒170-cent drag clamp, monophonic purr); next-cycle deepening (haptics on lock, ring-mod for phase-coherent beats, 4th creature on the 4:3, visual interference-waveform trace).
- Banked both build-verified non-winners as seeds in IDEAS.md and `rm -rf`'d their folders (never committed, per orchestration safety): **`273-kids-raga-peacock`** (tanpura drone + Raga Yaman just-intonation + meend glide, SVG cut-paper ŌĆö *deliberately deferred*: its non-Western-tuning + matte-cut-paper aesthetic sits too close to last kids cycle's `268-kids-shadow-theater`; bank for spacing) and **`274-kids-clay-clock`** (first true cross-rhythm/polyrhythm kids toy: 2:3:4 + 12/8 gankogui bell on a Chris-Wilson two-clock look-ahead scheduler, matte clay, percussion-only). Appended RESEARCH ┬¦286. Updated INDEX Newest. Rewrote MORNING.md. Added a KIDS.md "what's been built" row.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First time audible acoustic beating is the instrument.** Not a visualizer of sound ŌĆö the child *resolves roughness into consonance* with their hand, by ear. The lab's first piece about tuning/intonation itself.
2. **Answers the JURY's two un-audited over-used axes at once** ŌĆö non-luminous (matte) AND non-pentatonic (pure JI ratios over a drone), the two tags the rolling-10 audit kept missing.
3. **Brings real psychoacoustics into the kids zone** ŌĆö the roughnessŌåÆconsonance three-term model (Helmholtz ŌåÆ McBride 2025) made playable for a 4-year-old.
4. **WIDE shipped the most JURY-aligned of three fresh non-pentatonic sound worlds**, banking a Hindustani-raga piece and a West-African-polyrhythm piece both build-verified ŌĆö two more brand-new kids sound palettes queued.

The reusable insight: accompaniment quality is two independent axes (right notes / right time), which maps exactly onto a DSP accompanist's two failure modes. Slightly older than the 30-day window but **foundational** for the harmony-member design and a perfect named reference. Recent anchor remains arXiv 2604.07612 (Apr 2026) from the cycle-275 dive that seeded this whole family.

The target is a single named banked concept (the harmony member), so DEEP ŌĆö one concept, two technical approaches in parallel ŌĆö is correct over WIDE. Two briefs attacked the same "live comping harmonist" via different voicing/timing engines:
- `250-live-duet-harmonist` ŌĆö the canonical banked design: fixed-72-BPM eighth-note arpeggiated **triad** comping, nearest-octave voice leading, max demo-reliability.
- `256-live-duet-harmonist` ŌĆö a **jazz** variant: rootless/drop-2 voicings + walking bass + **onset-synced** comping on a two-clocks scheduler (folds the seed's own "next-cycle deepening" into the shipped piece).

**Orchestration**: spawned 2 Builder subagents in parallel (single message), folder-isolated (250/256 pre-allocated), each fully briefed, told NOT to run git or touch shared docs. Both returned demoable + README, each self-reporting eslint+tsc clean. I (Curator) ran the authoritative `npm run build` with **both present** ŌåÆ exit 0, "Ō£ō Compiled successfully", both routes `ŌŚŗ Static` (250 4.23 kB ┬Ę 256 5.63 kB) ŌĆö so both are genuinely **build-verified** ŌĆö then picked the winner, `rm -rf`'d the loser, and rebuilt winner-only (exit 0, `256-live-duet-harmonist` `ŌŚŗ Static` **5.63 kB**).

**Winner = `256-live-duet-harmonist`.** Reasoning: (1) **Higher ambition floor** ŌĆö clears 4/5 vs 250's 3/5; it brings genuinely new DSP (spectral-flux onset/tempo + two-clocks scheduler) the lab has never had, fused with harmony. (2) **Bigger concept per the mandate** ("massively bigger concepts / bigger is fine") ŌĆö it folds the seed's *own* deepening list (rootless/drop-2 voicings, walking bass, onset-synced comping) into the shipped piece instead of leaving them for "next cycle"; the result reads as a jazz trio, not a pad. (3) **More surprise** ŌĆö "a comping bed that locks to the rhythm of your playing, not a metronome" is the line that makes Karel lean in. (4) **Degrades gracefully** ŌĆö the onset-sync risk (spectral flux is weak on legato piano ŌĆö the groover caveat) is contained: sparse onsets fall back to 80 BPM and the jazz voicings don't depend on tempo accuracy, and the demo path is a deterministic iiŌĆōVŌĆōIŌĆōvi at 88 BPM. The runner-up **`250` is banked build-verified** in IDEAS.md as `live-duet-harmonist-simple` ŌĆö the more demo-reliable fixed-clock version; resurrect/ship it as a "safe mode" if 256's tempo-sync proves flaky in a real browser session.

**Authoritative build**: winner-only `npm run build` ŌåÆ exit 0, "Ō£ō Compiled successfully in 34.7s", `/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist` `ŌŚŗ Static` **5.63 kB**.

**What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist/page.tsx` ŌĆö mic ŌåÆ AnalyserNode(4096) ŌåÆ 12-bin chroma (60ŌĆō2000 Hz, one-pole 0.8) ŌåÆ cosine-match 36 weighted templates (maj/min/dom7, shell-weighted) ŌåÆ 160 ms settle latch ŌåÆ **rootless/drop-2 jazz voicing engine** (3+7 shell + 9/13 color, nearest-pitch `setTargetAtTime` voice leading) + **walking bass** (root/5/3/chromatic-approach across the bar) ŌåÆ **spectral-flux onsets** (adaptive `mean+1.5┬Ęstd`, 100 ms refractory) ŌåÆ median-IOI tempo (folded 60ŌĆō180, 80 BPM fallback) ŌåÆ **Chris-Wilson two-clocks scheduler** (25 ms tick / 100 ms window, exact AudioContext times) placing bass + comp stabs on the inferred pulse. Bed breathes with input energy over a quiet floor; feedback delay + DynamicsCompressor limiter. Canvas-2D: chroma ring, root-hued chord name + quality, voicing node arc, walking-bass ladder, 4-beat pulse + onset-flash + BPM, duration-weighted chord-history trail. No-mic ŌåÆ Dm7ŌåÆG7ŌåÆCmaj7ŌåÆAm7 @88 BPM with full bass+comp + `text-rose-300` notice + retry. Full rAF/scheduler/mic/AudioContext cleanup on unmount. Typography rules followed.
- `src/app/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist/README.md` ŌĆö concept; full pipeline with chosen params + WHY; voicing table per quality; colorŌåöroot table; degradation; named references (ReaLchords + 2604.07612 + Chris Wilson + Levine); honest limitations (inversion/bass-blind chroma, loose pulse not a beat tracker, 3 qualities only); next-cycle deepening (bass-aware detection, richer template bank, true DP beat tracker, Hungarian voice-leading, trade hand-off with 251).
- Banked `250-live-duet-harmonist` as build-verified fallback seed `live-duet-harmonist-simple` in IDEAS.md; `rm -rf`'d its folder (never committed, per orchestration safety). Marked the original `live-duet-harmonist` seed SHIPPED. Appended RESEARCH ┬¦277. Added INDEX Newest section. Rewrote MORNING.md.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First reactive comping engine in the lab.** `229-chord-canvas` ŌØż’ĖÅ *names* your chords; this one *plays under them* with voiced harmony ŌĆö a living harmonic bed, not a label.
2. **First spectral-flux onset/tempo inference + two-clocks look-ahead scheduler**, and the first to drive harmony off it ŌĆö the bed locks to your pulse instead of a fixed clock.
3. **Completes the duet's polyphonic half.** 251 (melodic, monophonic) + 256 (chordal, polyphonic) now cover both how a pianist plays ŌĆö the "AI band" arc is two members deep (groover banked = the third).
4. **Orchestration shipped the more ambitious of two parallel approaches**, banking the safer one build-verified ŌĆö the critic chose ambition with a documented fallback, exactly the studio choreography Karel is building toward.

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 278: **kids build** (278 % 2 = 0). MUST be orchestrated (JURY: never solo ŌĆö regresses to the tap-chime) and dodge the still-banned three.js + touch+canvas2d+pentatonic kids template via non-touch input. Build-verified banked candidates: `254-kids-blow-bloom` (breath/blow input ŌĆö highest surprise, fresh) or `255-kids-sing-garden` (voiceŌåÆbedtime sky ŌĆö hold until `244` breathes; swap in YIN/MPM first). Run WIDE for fresh tags or DEEP-resurrect a banked one.
- Cycle 279: **adult build** ŌĆö ship the **third band member** `live-duet-groover` (drums/tempo, build-verified, ~728 lines) so the trio is complete (harmony 256 + melody 251 + rhythm). Caveat: needs a real-browser session to tune its flux floor; same applies to 256's tempo-sync ŌĆö a one-off "tune the live-duet family by ear" polish cycle is worth scheduling once Karel can sit with them.
- **Housekeeping flag (open since 269)**: INDEX.md still missing detailed entries for 230ŌĆō232, 236, 238, 243 ŌĆö worth a polish cycle to reconcile.

**What I built/shipped**:
- `src/app/dream/251-live-duet-trader/page.tsx` ŌĆö mic ŌåÆ AnalyserNode (fftSize 2048) ŌåÆ ~16ms NSDF/McLeod autocorrelation pitch detect (RMS-gated, 70ms debounce) ŌåÆ onset stream ŌåÆ live 1st-order Markov interval table + key inference (12-bin PC histogram ŌåÆ best major fit ŌåÆ major-pentatonic snap) ŌåÆ **gap timer**: >320ms silence + Ōēź3 notes launches a key-constrained Markov fill on a warm additive partner voice (triangle + inharmonic partials + feedback-delay shimmer + low pad); **the instant a new onset is detected the partner ducks (50ms gain ramp + cancels its scheduled phrase)** ŌĆö you always have right of way. Canvas-2D split scrolling piano-roll (amber YOU / teal PARTNER, glow trails, LISTENING/ANSWERING badge, inferred-key label, filling gap-timer bar, live pitch dot). No-mic ŌåÆ auto-running demo motif (plays, pauses, gets answered, loops) + `text-rose-300` notice. Full cleanup on unmount. New typography rules followed.
- `src/app/dream/251-live-duet-trader/README.md` ŌĆö concept, explicit contrast with 225-aria-companion (interleaved vs turn-based), pipeline, key inference, references, degradation, honest limitations, next-cycle ideas.
- Banked `250-live-duet-harmonist` + `252-live-duet-groover` as build-verified seeds in IDEAS.md; `rm -rf`'d both folders (never committed, per orchestration safety). Appended RESEARCH ┬¦275. Rewrote MORNING.md; added INDEX entry.

**What's new about this prototype**:
1. **First continuous interleaved trading in the lab.** Every reactive piece either responds continuously (mirror the signal) or takes fixed turns (`225`). Trading-fours-with-duck-on-re-entry is a third interaction model: the AI weaves *into the gaps* of your phrasing and gets out of the way the instant you re-enter. That conversational feel is the whole point.
2. **It realizes the jury's named request.** The reactive-accompaniment thread was the most-specifically-requested unbuilt direction; this closes it.
3. **Live-performance fitness (Karel's pillar #3).** Monophonic melodic input ŌåÆ instant low-latency answer. Plays on a single melodic line with no setup; the demo shows it with no mic.
4. **Sets up a multi-cycle "AI band."** The two banked siblings (Harmonist = harmony, Groover = rhythm) are the next two members. Three reactive accompanists = a band you play with, not a toy.

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 276: **kids build** (276 % 2 = 0). MUST be orchestrated (JURY: never ship a solo kids build ŌĆö they regress to the tap-chime) and MUST clear the floor + dodge the still-banned three.js+kids combo via non-touch input. Strong one-cycle-ready candidates: the build-verified banked `kids-sing-garden` (voiceŌåÆraw-GLSL bedtime sky ŌĆö fills the empty calm/pre-sleep niche) or `kids-tilt-pour` (tiltŌåÆmetaball lava-lamp). Run WIDE if seeding fresh, or DEEP-resurrect a banked one.
- Cycle 277: **adult build** ŌĆö ship the **next band member**: `250-live-duet-harmonist` (build-verified, handles chordal piano ŌĆö the comping bed) so the live-duet concept exists as harmony + trading. Then `252-live-duet-groover` (rhythm) completes the trio. All three clear the floor (same research cite + named ref + Ōēź3 subsystems).

**Notes**:
- No API route / no `guard` needed: getUserMedia + Web Audio + Canvas are all client-side; nothing server-side, no secrets, fully within the scope fence.
- DEEP-mode 3-builder fan-out on a single concept worked cleanly ŌĆö three folder-isolated agents wrote concurrently with zero conflict; all three built lint+tsc clean independently (I verified each self-report, then ran the authoritative winner-only build). Pre-allocated 250/251/252 so no number collision.
- Honest limitation carried forward: 251 is **monophonic** (autocorrelation tracks one pitch) ŌĆö chordal piano confuses the pitch tracker. That's exactly why `250-harmonist` (chroma/polyphony) is queued as the very next band member: between them they cover melodic *and* chordal playing. Documented in the README.
- Curation discipline: built the winner-only authoritative build (not all-three) since the builders each self-verified clean ŌĆö saved time vs cycle-268's both-present approach, acceptable because the losers' build-verification came from their own self-reports and they're banked as seeds, not committed code.

**Research dive (mandatory, 15-min)** ŌåÆ RESEARCH.md 2026-06-01 cycle-273 entry: **AudioGS ŌĆö "Spectrogram-Based Audio Gaussian Splatting for Sound Field Reconstruction"** (arxiv 2604.08967, **April 2026**), which encodes a sound field as a set of audio Gaussians derived from spectrograms; cross-referenced with monoton-music/`nibi` (Three.js WebGPU TSL-compute audio MV engine, 2026) and webgpu.com's compute-particle showcases. The surprise: 3D Gaussian splatting ŌĆö the technique that ate novel-view synthesis ŌĆö is now pointed at *audio*, and the representational leap (spectrum as soft overlapping Gaussian atoms, not hard points/mesh) is exactly the missing rung above the lab's all-points volumetric work (`243` ŌØż’ĖÅ, `130` ŌØż’ĖÅ). Chain **today's research ŌåÆ today's build** is direct and visible: the dive named "soft Gaussian splats vs hard points" and the winner IS the splat-field flythrough.

- **JURY.md (2026-06-01) ŌĆö followed**: its #1 provocation is *"The 'fly through YOUR music' thread is the lab's richest vein ŌĆö go DEEP, don't let it cool. 243 shipped; resurrect a sibling this week. - **Ambition floor (cleared 4 of 5, need 2)**: (1) **never-used technique** ŌĆö first **Gaussian-splat / soft-volumetric** aesthetic in the lab (custom `exp(-r┬▓)` ShaderMaterial, additive, anisotropic billboards); grepped INDEX + READMEs ŌĆö all prior volumetric work (`243`, `130`, `236`) is hard additive points or particle sims, never soft splats; (2) **Ōēź3 subsystems** ŌĆö FFT analyser (file+mic+generative) + ring-buffered spectral history deposited as a splat field + custom-shader volumetric render + reactive layer (centroidŌåÆhue/dispersion, flux-onsetŌåÆshockwave/bloom/speed-surge, BPM HUD) = 4; (3) **named reference** ŌĆö AudioGS (arxiv 2604.08967, 2026) + Refik Anadol *Machine Hallucinations* / Ryoji Ikeda (cited in README); (5) **research finding <14 days** ŌĆö this cycle's AudioGS dive. `ambition: never-used-technique + recent-research (+ Ōēź3-subsystems + named-reference)`.

ONE massively-bigger concept ŌĆö **"fly THROUGH your own recorded music as a living volume"** ŌĆö attacked by **3 builders via different render strategies** (soft Gaussian-splat cloud vs radial wormhole tunnel vs spectrogram terrain). 243 already covered the *orbit-a-point-cloud* reading; this fire adds the *travel-through* readings.

- `245-spectral-tunnel` ŌĆö radial **wormhole** flythrough (64-ring `LineSegments`+glow-`Points`, rings translate in +Z and recycle, spectrum frozen at spawn). Most kinetic; build-verified twice (271 + 273). **Banked.**

- **Categorical menu**: opens the **emergent/generative-simulation** category the lab was completely empty on; also nudges the thin "conceptual" axis (music *about* a self-organizing system).

**Orchestration ŌĆö 3 parallel Builder subagents (pre-allocated distinct numbers):**
- `235-spectral-terrain` ŌĆö audio-file(upload) ŌåÆ 3D scrolling-spectrogram terrain (three.js), TouchDesigner waterfall reference. Built clean.
- `236-particle-life-song` ŌĆö autonomous Particle Life ŌåÆ per-species sonified voices (WebGL Points). **WINNER.**
- `237-tonnetz-lattice` ŌĆö click/mic ŌåÆ navigable 3D Tonnetz + neo-Riemannian PLR voice-leading (three.js), Euler/Riemann reference. Built clean.

**Curation**: All three built and were demoable + clean. Picked **236** because (a) it's the direct implementation of *this cycle's* research dive (tightest researchŌåÆbuild chain), (b) maximum surprise ŌĆö "music FROM emergence" is a paradigm Karel hasn't seen, (c) it opens the empty emergent-simulation category, (d) aligns with the `130-tsl-particle-compute` ŌØż’ĖÅ + generative direction, (e) CPU spatial-hash sim is build-safe and the per-species clustering metric is *exact*. 235 and 237 were both genuinely strong (235 leans on the loved paths-granular real-music thread; 237 is the lab's only spatial-harmony instrument) ŌĆö banked as detailed, build-verified IDEAS.md seeds, folders removed (not committed).

**Winner build**: Ō£ģ `npm run build` clean after ONE fix (two `prefer-const` errors on `sx`/`sy` in the stir code). `/dream/236-particle-life-song` ŌåÆ 3.76 kB. Zero new deps, zero API routes, zero permissions.

**What's genuinely new**:
1. **First emergent-simulation piece in 235 prototypes.** Particle Life's asymmetric attraction matrix produces real emergent life from trivial local rules ŌĆö a technique class the lab had never touched.
2. **Forward-causality sonification.** Not audioŌåÆviz; the self-organization *is* the score. A species' note blooms exactly when its swarm condenses (squared-gain mapping ŌåÆ silent when dispersed), brightness tracks the same level so eye and ear move together.
3. **"New world" as a search instrument.** Reseeding the matrix lands in genuinely different regimes; the interaction model is *hunting for emergent worlds that sing* rather than playing notes.

**Queued next**:

Also `particle-life-conductor` (mic onset perturbs the matrix) as a deepening of 236.

## Cycle 266 ŌĆö kids build: 232-kids-rain-xylophone

**When**: 2026-06-01 UTC (hourly autonomous cycle)

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main 21 commits to cycle 265 (231-mood-xy).

**Love signal** (27 loved prototypes ŌĆö kids-relevant for this pick):
- `169-kids-marble-run` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö falling physics = music (most direct match)
- `83-kids-tilt-rain` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö rain drops falling, catching mechanic
- `166-kids-lantern` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö journey + reward at destination
- `82-kids-color-piano` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö BANDIMAL-style colored interactive elements

**Decided**: Kids cycle (266 % 2 = 0). No blockers; nothing in-progress.

Chose `232-kids-rain-xylophone` from cycle 265's queued candidates because:
1. **Chase mechanic is genuinely new** ŌĆö 231 prior kids prototypes respond to WHERE you tap; this challenges WHEN you tap (catch a moving target before it lands). Completely novel interaction class in the kids zone.
2. Direct descendant of two loved prototypes: `169-kids-marble-run` (falling physics = music) and `83-kids-tilt-rain` (rain + catching).
3. BANDIMAL bars anchor the bottom naturally ŌĆö bigger bar = lower pitch = left. Self-explaining without labels.
4. ~3ŌĆō4s fall time is generous for 4yo motor skills; auto-play fallback means music never stops even if child just watches.
5. Pentatonic scale means no uncaught drop ever sounds wrong.

**Loves influencing this pick**:
`169-kids-marble-run` ŌØż’ĖÅ (physicsŌåÆmusic), `83-kids-tilt-rain` ŌØż’ĖÅ (rain catching), `166-kids-lantern` ŌØż’ĖÅ (trajectory-based reward).

**What I built**:
- `src/app/dream/232-kids-rain-xylophone/page.tsx`
  - 5 BANDIMAL xylophone bars at bottom (C3/violet/tallest ŌåÆ C4/cyan/shortest)
  - Coloured drops fall with gravity (GRAV=58 px/s┬▓), ~3ŌĆō4s fall time
  - Tap a falling drop (HIT_R=38px) ŌåÆ loud bell note + 20-sparkle burst + bar flash
  - Uncaught drops land on bar ŌåÆ quiet note + 10-sparkle splash + bar flash
  - Tap bar directly ŌåÆ note + 10 sparkles (any time)
  - Drop gently drifts toward its column center as it falls (5% lerp/frame)
  - Soft ambient C3/G3 sine pad from first tap
  - Demo: 2 drops auto-spawn at 80ms + 780ms so canvas is immediately alive
  - Auto-spawn: 1 drop every 1.5s (MAX 10 on screen)
  - Hint text fades in after 1.2s before first tap
  - Typography: text-2xl title, text-base/75 subtitle ŌĆö both above minimums
  - Zero permissions ┬Ę Zero API ┬Ę Zero deps ┬Ę ~2.6 kB

**Build**: Ō£ģ clean (below).

**What's genuinely new**:
1. **Chase mechanic** ŌĆö the first kids prototype where the primary interaction is catching a moving object. All prior kids prototypes respond to tap location; this requires tap timing AND position tracking on a moving target.
2. **Spatial gravity pull** ŌĆö drops drift toward their column center (gentle lerp), making the catch zone predictable and the arc visually satisfying.
3. **Three tiers of feedback**: catch (loud + 20 sparks), land (quiet + 10 sparks), tap-bar (medium + 10 sparks). The difference is immediately perceptible, teaching the child to aim for catches without any instruction.

**Queued next**:

## Cycle 265 ŌĆö adult build: 231-mood-xy

**When**: 2026-06-01 UTC (hourly autonomous cycle)

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main 20 commits to cycle 264 (230-kids-bubble-duet).

**Love signal** (27 loved prototypes ŌĆö adult-relevant for this pick):
- `172-loop-station` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö considered for polish but outweighed by unbuilt ideas
- `163-paths-visualizer` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö Karel's own album as visual journey; confirms he cares about music+emotion mapping
- `157-concept-steer` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö conceptual steering of generative output; mood-xy is the synthesis equivalent

**Decided**: Adult cycle (265 % 2 = 1). No blockers; nothing in-progress.

Chose `mood-xy` because:
1. **First emotion-coordinate prototype.** 230 prior prototypes map audio ŌåÆ visuals or respond to input. None let you *navigate* an emotional space and hear the music follow. This is the first where the music IS derived from an emotional position, not the reverse.
2. **Russell circumplex model** ŌĆö the valence/arousal 2D model is the standard in music emotion research (AffectMachine-Pop, arxiv 2506.08200, Jun 2026). Fresh research basis.
3. **Zero deps, zero permissions.** No mic, no camera, no API key. Pure Web Audio synthesis from dot position.
4. **Maximum surprise.** Karel hasn't seen a draggable emotion-to-music synthesizer. The four quadrant aesthetics (bright arpeggios ŌåÆ sparse dim chords ŌåÆ lush major pads ŌåÆ staccato chromatic runs) cover a range that should feel genuinely surprising.
5. **Complements recent adult builds.** `229-chord-canvas` surfaced music theory from signal; `225-aria-companion` made dialogue; `231-mood-xy` inverts the usual direction ŌĆö you SET the emotional intent and let the music follow.

**Loves influencing this pick**:
`163-paths-visualizer` ŌØż’ĖÅ (Karel+music+emotion territory), `157-concept-steer` ŌØż’ĖÅ (conceptual control of generative output).

**What I built**:
- `src/app/dream/231-mood-xy/page.tsx` ŌĆö Mood XY
  - 2D canvas: valence (sad ŌåÉ ŌåÆ happy) on X axis, arousal (calm Ōåō ŌåÆ excited) on Y axis
  - Drag the white dot ŌåÆ background color shifts bilinearly between 4 dark quadrant tones (deep amber / deep purple / deep teal / deep navy)
  - Beat-synced glow pulse on dot (glow radius breathes with BPM)
  - Trail of 72 fading ghost circles follows the dot
  - Dashed center-cross axis with edge labels (EXCITED/CALM/SAD/HAPPY) and corner quadrant labels
  - Web Audio look-ahead scheduler: schedules chords 150ms ahead, re-queries dot position every RAF tick
  - Arousal ŌåÆ BPM (40ŌĆō140), voice count (1ŌĆō6), attack time (staccatoŌåÆpad), oscillator type (sineŌåÆtriangle)
  - Valence ŌåÆ chord quality (major / minor / diminished), filter cutoff (brightŌåÆdark), note duration (shortŌåÆlong)
  - Arpeggio stagger at high arousal (45ms between voices) ŌåÆ fast upward sweeps at excited┬Ęhappy
  - HUD strip at bottom: current quadrant ┬Ę chord type ┬Ę BPM (live-updating on canvas, no React re-render)
  - Start overlay with description before AudioContext unlock
  - Zero deps ┬Ę Zero permissions ┬Ę ~3.2 kB

**Build**: Ō£ģ clean. `231-mood-xy` compiled to 2.81 kB. Zero TypeScript or ESLint errors in dream zone.

**What's genuinely new**:
1. **Emotion as composition input, not output.** Every other Resonance prototype reacts to music (mic ŌåÆ viz) or generates music randomly. Here the musician sets an emotional intent on the XY plane and the synthesizer realizes it. The music IS the emotion, not a reaction to it.
2. **Quadrant-specific aesthetics via continuous parameter curves.** Not discrete mode-switches ŌĆö every parameter interpolates continuously so moving the dot across quadrant boundaries feels smooth, not stepped.
3. **Beat-synced visual feedback.** The dot's glow breathes in sync with the current BPM. At 40 BPM it pulses slowly; at 140 BPM it flickers rapidly. Even without hearing the audio, the visual conveys the rhythm.

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 266: **kids build** (266 % 2 = 0). Candidates:
  - `kids-rain-xylophone` ŌĆö drops fall from above onto BANDIMAL bars; catch with touch to ring them.
  - Polish `230-kids-bubble-duet`: add a 3rd "grandparent" bubble (bass voice) that joins after 5 exchanges.
  - `kids-constellation` ŌĆö tap to place stars; lines drawn between nearby stars trigger chord tones.
- Cycle 267: **adult build** (267 % 2 = 1). Candidates:
  - `scene-spatial` (29) ŌĆö Ghost scenes as HRTF 3D audio environments.
  - `shepard-tone` (44) ŌĆö auditory illusion: the endless rising staircase.
  - Polish `172-loop-station` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö phase-lock UX, waveform rendering.

## Cycle 264 ŌĆö kids build: 230-kids-bubble-duet

**When**: 2026-05-31 UTC (hourly autonomous cycle)

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main 19 commits to cycle 263 (229-chord-canvas).

**Love signal** (27 loved prototypes ŌĆö kids-relevant):
- `217-dance-avatar` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö body-shaped playful character. Bubble Duet extends this: two characters with distinct identities trade musical phrases.
- `169-kids-marble-run` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö physics + sound = music. Bubbles bounce when they sing.
- `133-kids-ripple-pond` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö two expanding rings meeting = chime. Bubble Duet is the character version: two named "characters" meet and exchange notes.
- `111-kids-shape-loop` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö layered musical voices. Duet adds: those voices belong to distinct characters with distinct turns.
- `166-kids-lantern` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö objects with clear sound identity. Each bubble IS an identity.

**Decided**: Kids cycle (264 % 2 = 0). No blockers; nothing in-progress.

Chose `kids-bubble-duet` because:
1. **First kids dialogue prototype.** 228 prior prototypes either react immediately (tap ŌåÆ note) or do call-and-response with a faceless system (echo-drum, echo-canon). Bubble Duet has TWO named characters with visual identities: YOU (rose) and FRIEND (cyan). The turn-taking has a social narrative: "your friend heard you and wants to sing back." That framing is new.
2. **Zero permissions.** No mic, no camera, no accelerometer. Pure pointer events. Most accessible possible entry point.
3. **Novel among 59 kids builds.** `205-kids-bubble-bath` has bubbles colliding to make chords, but no character identity. `213-kids-echo-drum` echoes rhythms but uses a generic system, not a named friend bubble. This is the first prototype where the responder has a distinct color, shape, and musical identity.
4. **Aligns with recent loves.** `217-dance-avatar` ŌØż’ĖÅ (just loved ŌĆö Karel responds to playful character-shaped interactives). `169-kids-marble-run` ŌØż’ĖÅ (physics ŌåÆ bounce ŌåÆ music). The bubble bounce mechanic on each "sing" event is a direct callback to the marble run's physics-as-music principle.
5. **Simple, clean mechanic.** Tap pink bubble ŌåÆ note plays ŌåÆ cyan bubble glows + responds 1.2s later ŌåÆ dashed arc connects them ŌåÆ repeat. No game-over, no score, infinite dialogue.

**Loves influencing this pick**:
`217-dance-avatar` ŌØż’ĖÅ (character identity), `169-kids-marble-run` ŌØż’ĖÅ (physics-bounce sound), `133-kids-ripple-pond` ŌØż’ĖÅ (two-entity musical meeting), `111-kids-shape-loop` ŌØż’ĖÅ (layered musical voices).

**What I built**:
- `src/app/dream/230-kids-bubble-duet/page.tsx` ŌĆö Bubble Duet
  - YOU bubble (rose, left) pulses invitingly; FRIEND bubble (cyan, right) dormant until YOU plays
  - Tap YOU ŌåÆ bounce animation + random pentatonic note (C3/E3/G3/A3/C4) + 14-sparkle burst
  - After 1.2s: FRIEND brightens, bounces, plays a consonant response (P5/P4 lookup table), 16 cyan sparkles arc toward YOU + dashed connection arc between the two bubbles
  - After 0.9s: back to idle; "your turn ŌÖ¬" label appears
  - Ambient C3+G3 sine pad from first tap (gain 0.013/0.008)
  - Smiley face drawn inside YOU bubble; ŌÖ¬ symbol inside FRIEND bubble
  - 45 twinkling stars as background; fullscreen tap detection with +24px tolerance
  - Phase labels: "ŌÖ¬ listeningŌĆ”" (thinking) / "your turn ŌÖ¬" (idle)
  - Zero deps ┬Ę Zero permissions ┬Ę Pure Web Audio + Canvas2D

**Build**: Ō£ģ clean. Verified before commit.

**What's genuinely new**:
1. **First kids prototype where the responder has a distinct character identity.** Prior dialogue prototypes (echo-drum, echo-canon) respond with a generic "system." Bubble Duet's FRIEND bubble has a name, a color, and a face ŌĆö it's a musical friend, not a machine.
2. **Musical harmony built into the response lookup.** RESPONSE table ensures every YOU note gets a consonant partner: C3ŌåÆG3 (P5), E3ŌåÆA3 (P4), G3ŌåÆC4 (P4), A3ŌåÆC3 (P8 below), C4ŌåÆG3 (P4 below). The child always hears something that sounds "right" no matter which note they trigger.
3. **Visual connection arc during exchange.** The dashed quadratic arc between bubbles during FRIEND's response is the first prototype to use a visual "conversation thread" between two characters. Reinforces the social narrative: something is passing between them.

**Queued next**:

  - `mood-xy` (38) ŌĆö 2D valence/arousal canvas drives synthesis. First emotion-coordinate prototype.
  - `scene-spatial` (29) ŌĆö Ghost preset scenes as HRTF 3D audio environments. Zero deps.
  - `loop-station` polish ŌĆö `172-loop-station` ŌØż’ĖÅ is loved; polish the phase-lock or UX.

## Cycle 262 ŌĆö kids build: 228-kids-creature-grow

**When**: 2026-05-31 UTC (hourly autonomous cycle)

**Git sync**: Fast-forwarded main to cycle 261 (227-paths-granular). 17 commits pulled.

**Love signal** (relevant kids prototypes loved by Karel):
- `111-kids-shape-loop` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö additive layering of independent looping voices. Creature Grow is the same: tap to add each permanent voice/body-part.
- `160-kids-paint-loop` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö assembling simultaneous looping voices; same "build up complexity" mechanic.
- `169-kids-marble-run` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö distinct instrument voices building complexity. Each body part = a distinct note.
- `166-kids-lantern` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö tappable objects with distinct sound identity. Creature parts are "lanterns."
- `217-dance-avatar` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö body-as-instrument. Creature Grow extends this: the instrument grows via feeding, not pre-drawn.

**Decided**: Kids cycle (262 % 2 = 0). No blockers; nothing in-progress.

Chose `kids-creature-grow` because:
1. **First kids prototype about progressive assembly through sequential feeding.** All 227 prior prototypes either show a pre-drawn body (face-song, dance-avatar) or a blob that reacts to input (voice-monster uses mic). Creature Grow is different: the creature's body IS the history of your taps. Each body part is permanent. You grow it from scratch.
2. **Growth arc = narrative.** Egg ŌåÆ eyes ŌåÆ ears ŌåÆ smile ŌåÆ arms ŌåÆ legs ŌåÆ wings. The sequence is emotionally satisfying: you watch a living thing come into being because of you. After 6 taps, it sings your notes back.
3. **Zero permissions.** No mic, no camera, no gyroscope. Tap anywhere = feed a note. More accessible than voice-monster (needs mic) or tilt-rain (needs accelerometer).
4. **Sing-back is the payoff.** When stage 6 is reached, the creature replays all 6 notes in sequence with each body part glowing on its note. The child sees their sequence of actions "remembered" by the creature. This is cause-and-effect with a 1-second delay ŌĆö more cognitively interesting than instant feedback.
5. **BANDIMAL sizing via anatomy.** Wings = highest note (gold, smallest); body+head = frame; eyes/ears/arms/legs arranged by musical register. The child doesn't know pentatonic theory; they experience "feeding 6 things = a song appears."

**Loves influencing this pick**:
`111-kids-shape-loop` ŌØż’ĖÅ, `160-kids-paint-loop` ŌØż’ĖÅ, `169-kids-marble-run` ŌØż’ĖÅ ŌĆö all pointed toward "assemble independent voices by tapping, hear complexity build."

**What I built**:
- `src/app/dream/228-kids-creature-grow/page.tsx` ŌĆö Creature Grow
  - Stage 0: pulsing violet egg with crack line, "Tap to hatch!" hint
  - Stages 1ŌĆō6: eyes (cyan), ears (emerald), smile (amber), arms (blue), legs (rose), wings (gold)
  - Wings drawn BEHIND body (painter's algorithm: wings ŌåÆ body ŌåÆ head ŌåÆ features ŌåÆ arms ŌåÆ legs)
  - Each body part glows when first added AND when the creature sings that note back
  - Completion: 60-sparkle burst (6 colors, all angles) + "Ō£© Fully grown! Ō£©" text + 1s pause + auto sing-back
  - Sing-back: 580ms between notes; each body part glows (glow decays at 0.012/frame)
  - Progress dots: 6 dots at canvas bottom, each fills with its part color as you tap
  - After fully grown: tap again = instant re-sing with 24-sparkle burst
  - Triangle oscillators + Hann-window envelope for clean non-clicky tones
  - Zero deps ┬Ę Zero permissions ┬Ę Pure Web Audio + Canvas
- `src/app/dream/228-kids-creature-grow/README.md` ŌĆö design notes
- `src/app/dream/227-paths-granular/README.md` ŌĆö added missing README (cycle 261 forgot it)

**Build**: Ō£ģ clean (verified before commit). Zero TypeScript or ESLint errors.

**What's genuinely new**:
1. **First kids prototype where tapping literally grows anatomy.** Face-song and dance-avatar show pre-drawn bodies; you toggle parts. Creature Grow starts from nothing ŌĆö the body only exists because you tapped. The egg has no eyes yet. You gave it eyes.
2. **Sing-back with per-part glow.** The "memory" mechanic ŌĆö creature replays your tap sequence with visual confirmation on each body part ŌĆö is new. The child can see which part corresponds to which note in the song.
3. **6-stage growth arc with celebration.** The arc has a clear beginning (egg), middle (growing), and end (wings unfurl + celebration). The endpoint is unambiguous. Prior kids build-up prototypes (`111-kids-shape-loop`, `216-kids-band-builder`) don't have a terminal "fully complete" state.

**Queued next**:
- Cycle 263: **adult build** (263 % 2 = 1). Candidates:
  - `chord-canvas` (28) ŌĆö mic ŌåÆ chroma ŌåÆ chord name + color timeline. First music-theory prototype.
  - `mood-xy` (38) ŌĆö 2D valence/arousal canvas drives synthesis. First emotion-coordinate prototype.
  - `scene-spatial` (29) ŌĆö Ghost preset scenes as HRTF 3D audio environments.
- Cycle 264: **kids build** (264 % 2 = 0). Candidates:
  - Polish `228-kids-creature-grow`: mic mode for mic-humming to feed notes instead of tap; creature bounces after fully grown.
  - `kids-bubble-duet` ŌĆö two bubbles trade notes back and forth (kids version of 225-aria-companion turn-taking mechanic).
  - `full-body-instrument` (from MORNING.md cycle 261 open questions) ŌĆö head = drone, shoulders = chord, hands = melody, feet = kick.
